title: Update frontmatter parser to read v2.0 schema fields (id/type/maturity/layer/layer_reason/created_at)
description: Extend extractDescriptionFromFrontmatter at rule-meta-builder.ts:748-762 to parse the 6 new flat scalar fields defined in TASK-002 schema. The hand-rolled regex parser at lines 764-785 (extractScalar + extractInlineArray) already supports flat scalars — we add 6 new extractScalar() calls + propagate fields to RuleDescription. Add parser-level validation: id format regex, type/maturity/layer enum membership, created_at ISO 8601 shape. Invalid frontmatter logs warning and falls through to v1.x-compatible parse (no hard failure during transition). Does NOT change parser to gray-matter — flat scalar regex remains.

files.change: Add 6 extractScalar() calls in extractDescriptionFromFrontmatter for id/type/maturity/layer/layer_reason/created_at. Validate each via shared schema enums (KNOWLEDGE_TYPES, KNOWLEDGE_MATURITIES, KNOWLEDGE_LAYERS) before populating RuleDescription. Use formatKnowledgeId regex from TASK-002 to validate id format — if invalid, log warning via existing logger and treat as missing (preserves backward compat). Update RuleDescription TypeScript type in shared to include optional knowledge fields. Keep existing summary/intent_clues/tech_stack/impact/must_read_if/entities extraction unchanged.

When building agentsMetaNode, propagate parsed knowledge fields (id/type/maturity/layer) onto the node so they reach agents.meta.json and downstream consumers (doctor, plan-context). When stable_id is parsed from frontmatter id field (KP-/KT- format), set identity_source='declared' (works with TASK-003 changes).

implementation: Add 6 new const declarations using extractScalar(frontmatter, 'id')/etc.
Validate id via /^K[PT]-(D|P|G|M|PR)-\d+$/ regex; log warn + skip if invalid
Validate type/maturity/layer against KNOWLEDGE_TYPES/MATURITIES/LAYERS imported from shared
Validate created_at via Date.parse — log warn + skip if NaN
Extend RuleDescription return shape with optional knowledge fields
Update extractRuleDescription fallback path (line 731-737) to set knowledge fields to undefined consistently
In buildRuleMeta, when frontmatter.id matches KP-/KT- regex, pass it through to agentsMetaNode.stable_id with identity_source='declared'
Run existing rule-meta-builder.test.ts — all should still pass
Add 6+ new test fixtures for: valid full schema / valid minimal (only summary, all knowledge fields absent) / invalid id format / unknown type / malformed created_at / mismatched id-vs-layer

reference.examples: Mirror the existing pattern: `const id = extractScalar(frontmatter, 'id'); if (id !== undefined && !/^K[PT]-/.test(id)) { logger.warn(...); return undefined; }`. Per discussion Q4 decision: keep parser flat (no nested objects) — created_at is ISO 8601 string scalar, not date object.

criteria:
Frontmatter `id: KP-D-1
type: decision
maturity: validated
layer: personal
layer_reason: solo-dev preference
created_at: 2026-05-10T08:00:00Z` parses cleanly into RuleDescription with all 6 fields populated
Frontmatter missing knowledge fields (v1.x-style with only summary/intent_clues) parses without warning and produces RuleDescription with knowledge fields=undefined
Invalid id format `id: foo-bar-baz` triggers warn log and id field stays undefined (parsing does NOT throw)
Unknown type `type: opinion` triggers warn log; type field stays undefined
Mismatched id-vs-layer (`id: KP-D-1, layer: team`) triggers warn log; both fields stay undefined to avoid corrupt state
agents.meta.json node for parsed file has stable_id=KP-D-1 + identity_source='declared' (NOT path-derived)
All existing rule-meta-builder.test.ts tests pass unchanged
6+ new test fixtures cover positive + negative paths

rationale.chosen_approach: Additive scalar extraction reusing existing regex infrastructure; warn-and-skip on invalid input
alternatives_considered: Switch parser to gray-matter for full YAML (rejected — out of scope refactor)
Hard-fail on invalid frontmatter (rejected — would break in-flight v1.x files during transition)
decision_factors: Parser stability is a constraint (per code_anchors significance line)
Warn-and-skip enables gradual migration of legacy entries
Validation here is defense-in-depth against malformed scan output from TASK-007
rationale.tradeoffs: Silent fall-through on invalid fields could mask bugs; mitigated by visible warnings in logs and by TASK-006 doctor checks that flag missing fields

manual_checks: Parse a known-good v2.0 frontmatter fixture
Parse a known-good v1.x frontmatter fixture (regression)
Parse a malformed frontmatter, observe warn log
success_metrics: Parser handles 6 positive + 5 negative fixtures correctly
Zero regressions across existing 19 rule-meta-builder tests
